Below this, `left_products` array stores the cumulative product of elements to the left of each index in `nums`, starting with 1 (the product of zero elements).  Each element in `left_products` is calculated by multiplying the previous element by the corresponding element in `nums`. Similarly, `right_products` calculates the cumulative product of elements to the right of each index, starting from the rightmost element and moving left.  Asterisks (*) visually represent the multiplication operations between consecutive elements in `nums` to obtain `left_products`.  Double vertical lines (||) represent the multiplication between corresponding elements of `left_products` and `right_products` to produce the final `res` array, which contains the product of all elements in `nums` except the element at the current index.
